lesson: Warm runny product with the compressor running flat out and coils packed with ice means blocked airflow, not low gas. Defrost and clean — regassing fixes nothing.
symptoms: Product runny and warm — nowhere near frozen.
Bowl barely cold to the touch.

tests.error-log: TEMP HIGH alarm active. Very high compressor run time.
tests.temp-probe: Bowl at 8 °C. Suction line unusually cold and sweating heavily.
tests.inspect-beater: Evaporator coils behind the bowl are solid with ice. Airflow completely blocked.

symptoms: Slush turned to sweet soup by noon.

tests.temp-probe: Bowl at 9 °C. Suction line frosted solid.
tests.inspect-beater: Evaporator coils behind the bowl are a solid block of ice. No air moving at all.

symptoms: Product runny and warm with a whistling noise from the vents.

tests.temp-probe: Bowl 8 °C. Airflow across the coil nearly nil — the whistle is air fighting through an ice tunnel.
tests.inspect-beater: Coils iced over completely. Filter behind them matted with dust and damp.

flavour: The irony of a slushie machine dying from too much ice.
authenticityNote: Evaporator ice-over blocks heat exchange entirely; the compressor works harder and achieves nothing. Usually caused by a blocked air filter or a door left open allowing humid air in.
